name: Carnival of Chaos
source: The New Mordheimer, https://mordheimer.net/docs/warbands/grade-1a-warbands/carnival-of-chaos (fetched 2026-07-06). Grade 1a warband. Cited source: Empire in Flames (PDF). Owner should cross-check against their own copy of that supplement.

heroSlots.notes: 70 gc. Leader (6"). Wizard: uses Nurgle Rituals (see Magic).
60 gc, 0-2. Nurgle-bolstered strongmen. Unnatural Strength: starts the game already knowing the Strongman skill (Strength list).

25 gc + cost of Blessings, 0-2. Nurgle's Blessings: must start play with one or more Blessings of Nurgle, bought only at recruitment (see the warband's exclusive equipment) — first Blessing at listed cost, further ones on the same model cost double.

henchmenTypes.notes: 50 gc, 0-2. No weapons/armour — fights with filth-encrusted claws. Cloud of Flies: opponents in close combat suffer -1 to hit. Stream of Corruption: a 6" range shooting attack at Strength 3, no armour saves. Demonic: never gains Experience. Immune to Poison. Immune to Psychology (auto-passes Leadership-based tests). Cause Fear. Daemonic Aura: 5+ Armour save, modified by Strength as normal, negated by magic weapons/spells (attacks are also magical). Daemonic Instability: if taken Out of Action, banished (destroyed, no injury roll) on a D6 of 1-3; if the warband routs, must pass a Leadership test or be destroyed. statMaximums null: Daemons have no Human Maximum Profile.

15 gc. Swarm: unlike other Henchmen groups, may have more than 5 Nurglings in the group. No weapons/armour. Cloud of Flies, Demonic (never gains Experience), Immune to Poison, Immune to Psychology, Daemonic Aura (5+ save, negated by magic), Daemonic Instability — same as Plague Bearers. statMaximums null: Daemons have no Human Maximum Profile.
25 gc, any number. Rank-and-file Nurgle devotees.

120 gc, 0-1. TODO/unmodeled: the Plague Cart is a special "wagon" unit the source page splits into four separate sub-profiles rather than one M/WS/BS/S/T/W/I/A/Ld line, and refers to a separate 'wagons' mechanic page (movement, being destroyed piece-by-piece, etc.) that wasn't fetched — this app's data model only supports one stat line per unit, so statLine/statMaximums are left null rather than force-fit or guessed. Source profiles as given: Cart — W4; Wheel — W1; Horse — M8, S3, T3, W1, I3; Guardian — WS3, S3, T3, I3, A1 (the part that actually fights; no weapons/armour, no penalty for fighting unarmed). Rating impact not stated on the source page — TODO: verify whether it counts as a Large Creature (+20 flat) or a normal model (+5) for warband rating. Special rules: Plague Cart increases the warband's maximum warriors by +2, and lets Plague Bearers/Nurglings re-roll Instability Leadership tests and get +1 on Injury tests if taken Out of Action. Guardian is bonded to the cart (can never dismount/leave, can't be injured unless the Cart itself is destroyed, never gains Experience, its attacks cause Nurgle's Rot), and is Immune to Psychology (as is the Plague Cart itself).

exclusiveEquipment.name: Blessing of Nurgle: Stream of Corruption
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Blessing of Nurgle — Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. First Blessing at listed cost; further Blessings on the same model cost double.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: May spew forth a stream of maggots, entrails and filth: a shooting attack with a 6" range, resolved at Strength 3, no armour saves.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Blessing of Nurgle: Nurgle's Rot
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Blessing of Nurgle — Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. First Blessing at listed cost; further Blessings on the same model cost double.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Infected with Nurgle's Rot and immune to all poisons. If the Tainted One scores a to-wound roll of 6 in close combat, the target contracts the Rot (living targets only — Undead/Daemons/Possessed are immune). From then on, before each battle the infected model tests Toughness or permanently loses 1 Toughness (dying and being removed from the roster if it reaches 0); rolling a 6 on that test also passes the Rot to a random warband member.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Blessing of Nurgle: Cloud of Flies
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Blessing of Nurgle — Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. First Blessing at listed cost; further Blessings on the same model cost double.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Surrounded by a buzzing cloud of flies — the Tainted One's close combat opponent suffers -1 to hit.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Blessing of Nurgle: Bloated Foulness
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Blessing of Nurgle — Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. First Blessing at listed cost; further Blessings on the same model cost double.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Blessing of Nurgle: Mark of Nurgle
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Blessing of Nurgle — Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. First Blessing at listed cost; further Blessings on the same model cost double.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: +1 Wound and immune to all poisons.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Blessing of Nurgle: Hideous
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Blessing of Nurgle — Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. First Blessing at listed cost; further Blessings on the same model cost double.

specialRules: Once wandering gypsy players and thespians, the Carnival of Chaos are now cavorting daemon-worshippers of Nurgle, the Lord of Decay — colourful travelling entertainers by day, plague-bringing horrors by the 'Dance of Death' finale. Dangerous to Know: a Carnival of Chaos warband may never hire any Hired Sword. Tainted: counts as Possessed for purposes of Exploration and Serious Injuries.
